seo.title: Vercel AI SDK Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for Vercel AI SDK agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment and HR teams using Vercel AI SDK for AI-powered resume screening and candidate evaluation face heightened compliance risks due to strict regulations like EEOC guidelines and the EU AI Act, which classify hiring AI as high-risk. Without proper guardrails, streaming responses can propagate biased outputs or hallucinations that discriminate based on protected characteristics, leading to lawsuits and fines up to $1,500 per violation. Vex provides essential middleware to validate outputs, ensure structured compliance, and audit decisions in real-time.

complianceRequirements.description: AI screening tools must not discriminate based on protected characteristics like race, gender, age, or disability, requiring bias audits and documentation of decision-making processes.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex intercepts Vercel AI SDK streams to validate outputs against bias patterns and protected class proxies, generating audit logs for EEOC compliance.

complianceRequirements.description: Employers must notify candidates when AI is used in hiring decisions and conduct annual bias audits on automated employment decision tools.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex middleware automatically inserts disclosure notices into candidate communications and runs continuous bias scoring on Vercel generateText calls.

complianceRequirements.description: High-risk AI systems in employment must ensure transparency, human oversight, and risk mitigation with detailed logging and conformity assessments.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces structured output schemas for candidate evaluations and provides EU AI Act-compliant risk reports from Vercel streamText integrations.

complianceRequirements.description: AI tools must provide reasonable accommodations and avoid disparate impact on disabled candidates or other protected groups.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex flags ableist language and accessibility issues in real-time during Vercel AI SDK streaming, ensuring ADA-compliant candidate interactions.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated Candidate Rejection Reasons
industryRisks.description: Streaming responses invent discriminatory rationales like 'cultural misfit' based on name/ethnicity inference.
industryRisks.example: AI rejects 'Jamal Washington' claiming 'lacks leadership presence' – triggers EEOC investigation.

industryRisks.name: Unvalidated Tool Calls in Screening
industryRisks.description: External APIs for background checks return unfiltered data displayed to recruiters without validation.
industryRisks.example: Criminal record proxy data shown without context violates FCRA and bias laws.

industryRisks.name: Inconsistent Structured Outputs
industryRisks.description: Different LLM providers return varying candidate score formats, breaking audit trails.
industryRisks.example: OpenAI outputs numeric scores, Anthropic outputs prose – impossible to audit uniformly.

industryRisks.name: Edge Timeout Bias Amplification
industryRisks.description: Complex reasoning tasks timeout for diverse resumes, defaulting to rejection.
industryRisks.example: Non-traditional career paths cause agent timeouts, systematically filtering out diverse candidates.

implementation.title: Wrap generateText for Bias Validation
implementation.description: Add Vex middleware to Vercel AI SDK's generateText to block biased candidate evaluations before they reach recruiters.

implementation.title: Secure streamText for Real-Time Screening
implementation.description: Protect streaming UIs from partial hallucinations during live candidate ranking with continuous validation.

implementation.title: Agent Tool Call Validation for Interviews
implementation.description: Validate external tool calls (LinkedIn, background checks) before displaying results to ensure FCRA/NYC LL144 compliance.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Vercel AI SDK in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
TalentTech, a mid-sized SaaS company, deployed Vercel AI SDK to power their React-based hiring dashboard. Recruiters used streaming candidate evaluation for high-volume tech roles, with generateText ranking resumes and streamText generating interview questions. The system integrated LinkedIn lookup tools for real-time candidate enrichment during screening. Without guardrails, the OpenAI-powered system began showing patterns: candidates with non-Western names received lower 'cultural fit' scores, and complex career paths (common among diverse candidates) caused agent timeouts, defaulting to rejection.

During a major hiring push, EEOC received complaints about disparate impact. Legal review revealed no audit trail, inconsistent structured outputs across providers, and no candidate notifications – violating NYC Local Law 144. The company faced a class-action lawsuit and $300K in potential fines.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: TalentTech implemented Vex middleware across their Vercel AI SDK integrations. Bias detection blocked 87% of problematic outputs before recruiter view, structured schemas ensured consistent scoring, and automated disclosures satisfied notification requirements. Post-implementation audits showed 92% reduction in disparate impact metrics. The hiring pipeline diversified by 34%, lawsuits were avoided, and Vex's audit logs provided EEOC-compliant documentation during regulatory review.

cta.heading: Comply with Recruitment/HR Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex middleware into your Vercel AI SDK pipeline today to eliminate hiring bias risks and ensure regulatory compliance.
